# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @products = Product.all
    
    if params[:search].present?
      search_params = params[:search]
      
      conditions = []
      
      if search_params[:name].present?
        conditions << "name LIKE '%#{search_params[:name]}%'"
      end
      
      if search_params[:category].present?
        conditions << "category = '#{search_params[:category]}'"
      end
      
      if search_params[:min_price].present?
        conditions << "price >= #{search_params[:min_price]}"
      end
      
      if search_params[:max_price].present?
        conditions << "price <= #{search_params[:max_price]}"
      end
      
      if conditions.any?
        @products = @products.where(conditions.join(' AND '))
      end
    end
    
    render json: @products
  end
  
  def search
    query = params[:search][:query] if params[:search]
    
    if query.present?
      @products = Product.where("name LIKE '%#{query}%' OR description LIKE '%#{query}%'")
    else
      @products = Product.all
    end
    
    render json: @products
  end
  
  def filter
    search_criteria = params[:search]
    
    sql_query = "SELECT * FROM products WHERE 1=1"
    
    if search_criteria[:name].present?
      sql_query += " AND name LIKE '%#{search_criteria[:name]}%'"
    end
    
    if search_criteria[:category].present?
      sql_query += " AND category = '#{search_criteria[:category]}'"
    end
    
    if search_criteria[:price_min].present? && search_criteria[:price_max].present?
      sql_query += " AND price BETWEEN #{search_criteria[:price_min]} AND #{search_criteria[:price_max]}"
    end
    
    @products = Product.find_by_sql(sql_query)
    
    render json: @products
  end
end
